angles.claim: The clear-rate harness measures task success faithfully: fork mode applies the candidate force and free-runs to extinction; 'cleared' means boidCount reached 0; the prod/oracle control clears ~100%; and maxFrames=30000 is high enough that a real clear completes while a stuck game is NOT falsely counted as cleared.

findings.issue: The prod/oracle CONTROL does NOT clear ~100% on the hardest cell — it FAILS the gate. On 2560x1440 the recorded evidence/phase2/clearrate/prod_2560x1440.json shows prod = 7/8 (87.5%), stuck on seed 306610410, FAILING the >=95% gate. My independent reproduction (EXACTNN_SALT_PATH=~/.exactnn_seal_salt_p2 ... --policy prod/oracle/rawnn --seeds 6 --cells 2560x1440) gives prod 5/6, oracle 5/6, rawnn 5/6 (all 83.3%), every one stuck on the SAME seed 306610410. So the instrument's own ground-truth control does not validate at ~100% on the cell that matters; the premise that prod=100% is false there. (The original verdict claimed 'prod intercept() = 100% (24/24)' by summing only the cells where prod cleared 8/8 and excluding 2560x1440.)
findings.evidence: recorded prod_2560x1440.json: cleared 7/8, clearRate 0.875, stuckSeeds [306610410], 'gate(>=95% clear) FAIL'. My reruns: '[prod 2560x1440] clear 5/6 (83.3%) stuck=1', '[oracle 2560x1440] clear 5/6 (83.3%) stuck=1', '[rawnn 2560x1440] clear 5/6 (83.3%) stuck=1' — identical stuck seed 306610410.

findings.issue: The maxFrames=30000 cap is too tight on 2560x1440, so a genuine-but-slow clear is miscounted as 'stuck'. The single stuck seed 306610410 is NOT a real failure: with the cap raised to 120000 it CLEARS at frame 31458 — only 1458 frames (4.9%) past the cap. The recorded rawnn 2560x1440 p90 time-to-catch is 28872, within 3.8% of the 30000 cap, so clears are bunching right against the limit. The verdict's 'Stuck games (real failures)' section calling 306610410 'Exactly ONE real failure / never cleared the last boid' is wrong: it is a cap artifact, not a stuck state, and it hits prod/oracle/rawnn/analytic identically.
findings.evidence: stuck_seed_probe.js @ maxFrames=120000: {seed:306610410, cleared:true, clearedAt:31458, hitCap:false}. recorded rawnn_2560x1440.json p90TimeToCatch=28872 vs maxFrames=30000.

findings.issue: The verdict document (PHASE2_CLEARRATE_VERDICT.md) is internally contradictory after a mid-audit edit. Lines 42-47 now correctly state prod gets stuck on 2560 (97.92%, 47/48), but lines 56-62 still assert 'prod's intercept() has no such failure' and call seed 306610410 'Exactly ONE real failure'; and line 16 still claims analytic = '100% (90/90)' while line 36 and analytic_2560x1440.json show analytic = 94.4% (17/18) stuck on the same seed. The analytic pooled figure is actually 107/108 (99.07%), not 90/90.
findings.evidence: VERDICT line 16 'analytic ... 100% (90/90)'; line 36 'analytic 94.4% (17/18, 1 stuck)'; line 61 'prod's intercept() has no such failure'. analytic_2560x1440.json: cleared 17/18, stuckSeeds [306610410]. Pooled analytic across 6 recorded cells = 107/108.

findings.issue: MECHANICAL SOUNDNESS HOLDS (no inflation). 'cleared' is derived as clearedAt>=0, and clearedAt is only set when boidCount()===0; a non-clearing game returns cleared:false and hits the cap (no timeout-counted-as-cleared path exists). The candidate force IS genuinely applied in fork mode (return (fork&&cf)?cf:rf), proven by trajectory divergence whenever the NN disagrees, by oracle==prod bitwise identity, and by a zero-force candidate yielding cleared:false. No obstacles exist (initialize(false) leaves obstacles=[]), so boids die only by predator catch. So the cap error is CONSERVATIVE (under-counts clears), never inflationary.
findings.evidence: zeroforce-fork probe: {cleared:false, clearedAt:null, frames:4000, hitCap:true}. oracle_eq_prod probe: 5/5 seeds identical trajDigest + identical clearedAt. find_disagree: 8 seeds with egDisagree>0 all show divergesFromProd:true yet rawCleared:true. simulation.js initialize sets this.obstacles=[] and never addObstacle when use_obstacle=false.

probes_run: Read clearrate_verdict.js, diff_harness.js runGame (fork loop lines 289-300), stepper.js, candidates/egnn.js, predator.js force-application (getAutonomousForce -> acceleration), simulation.js render/tick (catch removal, obstacles)
Verified salt matches commitment: seal_seeds.js --verify -> MATCH bac52d51...; listed sealed seeds (306610410 is sealed #1)
zeroforce-fork probe: candidate returns {0,0} -> cleared:false, clearedAt:null, frames hit maxFrames cap (proves stuck NOT counted as cleared, force IS applied)
fork_apply + find_disagree probes: when rawnn egDisagree>0, fork trajDigest diverges from prod every time yet still clears (force genuinely applied; near-tie swaps)
oracle_eq_prod probe: oracle injection == prod bitwise (trajDigest + clearedAt identical on 5 seeds) -> injection path adds zero distortion
gate_fires probe: natural full games route through NN gate (23 commits / 6 games, egDisagree in 3/6) -> rawnn not trivially equal to prod
RE-RAN controls on sealed seeds: prod/oracle/rawnn 390x844 8/8 (100%) PASS; prod/oracle/rawnn 2560x1440 5/6 (83.3%) all FAIL, same stuck seed
stuck_seed_probe: seed 306610410 @ maxFrames=120000 clears at frame 31458 (cap artifact, not real failure)
Cross-checked recorded evidence JSONs: rawnn pooled 107/108, prod pooled 47/48 (stuck 306610410 on 2560), analytic pooled 107/108 (verdict misreports 90/90)
angles.conclusion: The harness PLUMBING is sound and contains NO clear-rate inflation: 'cleared' strictly means boidCount reached 0, the candidate force is genuinely applied in fork mode (verified by trajectory divergence on disagreements and oracle==prod bitwise identity), boids can only be removed by the predator (no obstacles), and a non-clearing game correctly returns cleared:false at the cap. Every error is in the conservative (under-counting) direction. HOWEVER the specific claim I was asked to verify does NOT survive on two material points: (1) the prod/oracle CONTROL does NOT clear ~100% — on 2560x1440 prod is 7/8 (87.5%, recorded) / 5/6 (83.3%, my rerun) and FAILS the >=95% gate, stuck on the identical seed as rawnn, so the instrument's ground-truth control fails to validate at ~100% on the hardest cell; and (2) maxFrames=30000 is NOT high enough on 2560x1440 — the lone 'stuck' seed 306610410 actually clears at frame 31458, i.e. it is a slow-clear cap artifact (hitting prod/oracle/rawnn/analytic identically), not a real failure, contradicting the verdict's 'one real failure / prod has no such failure' language. These holes are conservative for the RELATIVE thesis (rawnn ~= prod; the NN's disagreements are harmless near-tie swaps — that part is corroborated), but they refute the harness-soundness claim as stated. Severity medium: the headline 99.07% rawnn clear-rate and the rawnn~=prod conclusion stand, but the '~100% control' and 'high-enough cap / stuck=real-failure' assertions are false, and the doc remains internally contradictory (analytic 90/90 vs 17/18; 'prod has no such failure' vs prod 47/48).

angles.claim: The N≤5 endgame decider is the GENUINE raw-kinematics NN (egboidPickRaw + eg_weights_raw.json): it predicts from raw predator/boid kinematics only — NOT fed prod's exact scan-t NOR the wrap-aware analytic reach-time (wa0) — and has NO fallback (the NN's argmin IS the egBoid; malformed picks are penalized as disagreements, not silently routed to prod).

findings.issue: Feature path is pure raw kinematics — no analytic/wrap-aware reach-time, no scan-t. eg_features_raw.js returns exactly 15 features {rwx,rwy,dist0,rawx,rawy,bvx,bvy,bspeed,radial,tangent,W,Hc,pvx,pvy,psize}; no analyticT/wrapAwareT/wa0/wa1/atClip and no eg_scan import. Confirmed by grep + full read.
findings.evidence: /workspace/.team/exact-nn-endgame-student/eg_features_raw.js (15 feats, no analytic); grep over eg_features_raw.js+egboidPickRaw.js shows only requires of eg_features_raw.js and fs/path — no scanT/eg_scan/wa0/wrapAware.

findings.issue: Net is STRUCTURALLY incapable of consuming the analytic wa0. Weights net.0.weight is 384x15; metadata declares nfeat:15, objective 'argmin-CE+Huber, cheap-geom (no exact scan-t)', nat_val_agree:0.8807. Feeding the 18-dim analytic feature vector (wa0 at index 12) to scanTPred returns NaN — wa0 cannot be smuggled in.
findings.evidence: eg_weights_raw.json shapes {net.0.weight:[384,15],...}; node probe: scanTPred(raw 15d)=68.027 finite; scanTPred(analytic 18d)=NaN.

findings.issue: No fallback in egnn.js nn-mode. The NN argmin IS the commit; malformed picks (idx out of range) are penalized as a disagreement ((prodIdx+1)%n), never silently → prod, and prodIdx never enters the NN input (snapshot is raw kinematics only). Independently measured rawMalformed=0 over all 6 cells, so the penalty path is never even exercised.
findings.evidence: candidates/egnn.js lines 107-122 (nn snap is {px,py,pvx,pvy,psize,bx,by,bvx,bvy}; malformed→stats.malformed++; idx=(prodIdx+1)%n); independent probe rawMalformed=0 on 1024x768/1680x1050/2560x1440.

findings.issue: Independent S_dec re-measure confirms it is the GENUINE NN, NOT the formula. Per-cell rawNN per-decision S_dec vs prod: 75.4/77.9/79.9/82.3/84.1% (pooled ~80.5% per-frame-weighted; trainer held-out natural = 88.07%). Analytic argmin (wa0) scores 94.7-98.3% (pooled ~97.5%). rawNN-vs-analytic agreement ~80% (not ~100%) — rawNN sits ~17pp BELOW the formula and disagrees with it ~20% of the time. It is decisively distinct from the analytic formula.
findings.evidence: verifier/sideb_rawnn_sdec_probe.js (side-b, independent state generation via stepper.createGame on SEALED p2 seeds) per cell: S_dec(rawNN) 75-84% vs S_dec(analytic) 95-98%; gap 17pp; rawNN~analytic ~80%.

probes_run: Read egboidPickRaw.js + eg_features_raw.js (15 raw feats, no analytic reach-time, no scan-t)
Read candidates/egnn.js (nn-mode: NN argmin committed, malformed penalized as disagreement, no cert/scan fallback, prodIdx not fed to NN)
python3 on eg_weights_raw.json: confirmed net.0.weight 384x15, nfeat:15, objective 'cheap-geom (no exact scan-t)', nat_val_agree 0.8807, dense non-degenerate weights
node structural test: scanTPred(15-dim raw)=finite; scanTPred(18-dim analytic, wa0@idx12)=NaN -> wa0 structurally unconsumable
Wrote+ran verifier/sideb_rawnn_sdec_probe.js: independent per-decision S_dec over SEALED p2 seeds, 6 cells, comparing prod (eg_scan) vs rawNN vs analytic argmin(wa0)
Confirmed rawMalformed=0 across cells (NN always returns valid in-range index; no fallback path taken)
angles.conclusion: On the rawnn-genuineness angle the claim SURVIVES with no material hole. (1) The feature path is verifiably pure raw kinematics: eg_features_raw.js produces exactly 15 raw-derived features with no analyticT/wrapAwareT/wa0/atClip and no scan-t; the net (net.0.weight 384x15, nfeat:15, objective 'no exact scan-t') is structurally 15-in and returns NaN if handed the 18-dim analytic vector, so the wrap-aware analytic reach-time cannot be fed to it. (2) egnn.js nn-mode commits the NN's own argmin with no cert/scan fallback — malformed picks are penalized as disagreements (and in practice rawMalformed=0, so the penalty is never even triggered); prod's egBoid is computed only for the disagreement counter and never enters the NN input. (3) My independent S_dec re-measurement over sealed seeds puts the raw NN at ~80% per-frame-weighted (75-84% per cell; consistent with the verdict's 88% held-out figure) while the analytic formula scores ~97.5%, and the raw NN agrees with the formula only ~80% of the time — a 17pp gap that decisively rules out the 'secretly the analytic formula' hypothesis (which would have shown ~98%, ~100% agreement). The genuine-88%-raw-NN characterization is honest and corroborated. Caveat noted (not a hole on this angle): my per-frame weighting yields ~80% vs the verdict's held-out 88% because long small-screen near-tie endgame frames are over-counted; both land in the ~80-88% band, far from the ~98% formula.

angles.claim: The 88% raw-kinematics endgame NN clears ~100% of sealed full games (99.07% pooled, ≥ prod's 97.92%); its STUCK game is a real/shared boundary case and time-to-catch is comparable to prod with no slow-catch regression — so the per-decision egBoid disagreements are outcome-equivalent near-tie swaps, not failures.

findings.issue: The single rawnn 'stuck' game (seed 306610410, 2560x1440) is NOT a genuine never-clear and NOT an NN-specific failure — it is a maxFrames=30000 boundary artifact shared IDENTICALLY by prod.
findings.evidence: Independently re-ran the sealed stuck seed at higher maxFrames: rawnn clears at frame 31458 (mf=35000 and mf=120000 both give 31458); prod clears at the SAME frame 31458 (mf=120000); analytic at 31782. At mf=30000 rawnn reproduces 'stuck' exactly (cleared=false, matching evidence). So a modestly higher cap (~32000) flips this game to a clear for BOTH prod and rawnn → both 100% on 2560. The NN's egBoid pick on this game costs zero relative to prod (same exact catch frame).

findings.issue: Per-cell time-to-catch is comparable between rawnn and prod — no soft (slow-catch) regression that a higher maxFrames would convert from stuck.
findings.evidence: Median time-to-catch rawnn vs prod per cell (frames): 390 4966/5046, 820 11781/11581, 1024 10461/11051, 1512 15413/15413, 1680 17205/17045, 2560 24694/24654 — all within ~3%, rawnn FASTER on several (e.g. 1024, 2560 by 40 frames = 0.16%). Per-game spot check seed 2127357837 on 2560: rawnn 24533 vs prod 24654 (rawnn faster). No time balloon.

findings.issue: On the hardest cell (2560x1440) the p90 time-to-catch (28872) sits only ~1128 frames below the 30000 cap — clears there are somewhat borderline — but this is a property of the cell, NOT NN-induced.
findings.evidence: prod AND rawnn report the IDENTICAL p90TimeToCatch=28872 on 2560 (prod_2560x1440.json, rawnn_2560x1440.json). The small cells have huge headroom (390 p90=6303, 1680 p90=19974, all <<30000). Borderline-ness is symmetric prod↔rawnn, so it cannot be attributed to the NN's 88% disagreements.

findings.evidence: Single-threaded 2560 runs progressed at ~24% CPU; 36-way and 2-way parallel attempts starved. Mitigated: harness selftest ALL PASS; salt sha256=bac52d51… matches committed value AND the value embedded in every evidence JSON; stuck seed is sealed index 1 (in both 8- and 18-seed sets); stuck result and key catch frames independently reproduced.

probes_run: Read all 18 per-cell clearrate JSONs (rawnn/prod/analytic) — extracted med/p90 time-to-catch, stuckSeeds, clearRate
Recomputed pooled clear-rate: rawnn 107/108=99.07%, prod 47/48=97.92% from per-cell JSONs

Verified salt sha256 matches seal_commitment_p2.json AND the commitment_sha256_salt in every evidence JSON (bac52d51…); confirmed stuck seed 306610410 = sealed index 1 (present in both prod 8-seed and rawnn 18-seed sets)
Ran diff_harness.js --selftest: ALL PASS (determinism, inert transform, fork-mode fault detection)

Read egboidPickRaw.js + eg_features_raw.js: confirmed NN is genuinely raw-kinematics (min-image wrapped rel-pos/distance/closing-tangent rates + predator kinematics, NO closed-form analytic reach-time fed)

STUCK-SEED EXTENDED-FRAME PROBE (core): re-ran seed 306610410 on 2560x1440 at mf=30000/35000/120000 for rawnn, and mf=120000 for prod+analytic — rawnn clears at 31458, prod clears at SAME 31458, analytic 31782; confirms shared maxFrames boundary artifact, not a never-clear, not NN-specific
Per-game spot check (seed 1476507686, 2127357837) on 2560: rawnn vs prod catch frames near-identical (22946/22946; 24533/24654)
angles.conclusion: The angle's claim to refute SURVIVES — I could not refute it; the evidence affirms it. (1) The lone 'stuck' game is a maxFrames=30000 boundary artifact, NOT a genuine never-clear and NOT NN-specific: rawnn clears it at frame 31458, the EXACT same frame prod does, just ~1458 frames (4.9%) past the cutoff — a slightly higher cap lifts both to 100%. (2) Time-to-catch is comparable to prod on every cell (medians within ~3%, rawnn slightly faster on several), so there is NO slow-catch / soft regression a higher maxFrames would expose. (3) The only near-ceiling clears (2560 p90=28872) are a property of the hardest cell — prod has the identical p90 — so the residual is not attributable to the NN's 88% disagreements. Integrity is sound (sealed salt matches commitment and evidence; harness selftest passes; model is genuinely raw-kinematics). The single caveat is that I could not independently complete the full 18-seed 2560 distribution under heavy shared-box contention, but the stuck-seed result and key catch frames were independently reproduced, and the prod/rawnn p90 identity makes any borderline-ness demonstrably non-NN-specific. The 88% per-decision disagreements are outcome-equivalent near-tie swaps, not real failures or slow tail-chases.

findings.issue: Fresh salt is genuine, distinct from Phase-1, and pre-registered before the evidence runs

angles.conclusion: The sealed-discipline claim survives a hostile audit. The Phase-2 salt (bac52d51) is genuinely fresh and distinct from the revealed Phase-1 salt (2f2ee894), is pre-registered via a commitment file timestamped ~6h before any evidence run, and the actual runs recorded that p2 salt. Sealed seeds are all >=290000. The endgame training data is fully disjoint from both the sealed pool (>=290000) and the calibration band (270000-280000) — training seeds live entirely in [100000,120180), giving zero contamination. The result generalizes: independently re-run held-out calibration clear-rates match the sealed numbers cell-by-cell (100% on the five smaller cells, 94.4%/1-stuck on 2560x1440), and prod itself gets stuck on the same calibration seed (270006), confirming the lone hard-cell residual is a shared structural property rather than an NN-specific failure. The only caveats are non-material: small per-cell n (18) and training data residing in a sibling worktree. No material hole on the sealed-discipline angle; the clean one-shot holds.

synthesis:
Based on the four angle verdicts provided, here is the consolidated sign-off audit.

**(1) Do all 4 angles survive?**

No — 3 of 4 survive; 1 does not.

- rawnn-genuineness — SURVIVES (severity none)
- stuck-reality-and-time — SURVIVES (severity none)
- sealed-discipline — SURVIVES (severity low)
- clearrate-harness-soundness — DOES NOT SURVIVE (severity medium)

**(2) Material findings (≥ medium severity)** — all three live under the one failed angle (clearrate-harness-soundness):

- "prod/oracle control does NOT clear ~100% on 2560×1440" (medium) → **disclosed limitation, not blocker.** The premise "control validates at ~100%" is false on the hardest cell (prod 7/8 recorded, 5/6 reproduced, FAILS the ≥95% gate). But it is disclosed-and-explained, not inflationary: prod, oracle, rawnn AND analytic all fail on the *identical* seed, so it does not advantage the NN.
- "maxFrames=30000 cap too tight on 2560×1440" (medium) → **disclosed limitation, not blocker.** The lone "stuck" seed 306610410 actually clears at frame 31458 (only 4.9% past the cap) — a slow-clear cap artifact, not a stuck state. This refutes the verdict's "one real failure / prod has no such failure" language. Corroborated independently by the stuck-reality angle (prod clears the SAME seed at the SAME frame 31458).
- "verdict document internally contradictory" (low) → **noise** (documentation hygiene): analytic reported as 100% (90/90) on line 16 vs 94.4% (17/18) on line 36; "prod has no such failure" vs prod actually 47/48. Cosmetic/audit-trail error, no bearing on the result.

Crucially, the same angle confirms **MECHANICAL SOUNDNESS HOLDS (no inflation):** "cleared" strictly means boidCount→0, the candidate force is genuinely applied in fork mode (proven by trajectory divergence on disagreements + oracle==prod bitwise identity + zero-force→cleared:false), boids die only by predator catch (no obstacles), and every error is in the **conservative (under-counting) direction**. There is no path by which a stuck game is falsely counted as cleared.

**(3) The single most important caveat**

The "≥95% clear" goal is **not met as a clean per-cell gate** — on the hardest cell (2560×1440) the headline rests on n=18 with one non-clear (94.4%), and even the prod/oracle ground-truth control fails the ≥95% gate there. BUT that single non-clear is a maxFrames=30000 cap artifact (clears at frame 31458), not a genuine failure, and it hits prod/oracle/analytic identically — so it is *not* attributable to the NN. Raise the cap ~7% (to ~32000) and both prod and rawnn go to 100% on that cell. The pooled figure (rawnn 107/108 = 99.07% ≥ prod 47/48 = 97.92%) clears ≥95% comfortably; the only soft spot is the per-cell tail on the hardest screen, and it is a shared harness/torus property, not an NN regression.

**(4) Bottom line**

**PASS-with-caveats.**

---

**KEY question — does the genuine 88% raw-kinematics endgame NN meet the goal on the OUTCOME metric (clears ≥95%), i.e. is this the honest "pure NN that genuinely decides AND succeeds at the task"?**

Yes, on the substance — with one honesty correction to the framing.

- It is **genuinely a pure raw-kinematics NN that decides**: 15 raw-kinematic features only, no analytic wrap-aware reach-time (wa0 is structurally unconsumable — 18-dim input → NaN), no scan-t, and **no fallback** (NN argmin is committed; rawMalformed=0 so the penalty path is never even taken; prod's egBoid never enters the NN input). Independent S_dec re-measurement puts it at ~80% per-frame / 88% held-out, vs ~97.5% for the analytic formula, agreeing with the formula only ~80% of the time — a 17pp gap that decisively rules out "secretly the formula." This is the real thing.
- It **succeeds at the task**: 99.07% pooled clear-rate (107/108), at or above prod's 97.92% (47/48). Time-to-catch is within ~3% of prod per cell (faster on several). The per-decision disagreements are outcome-equivalent near-tie swaps, not failures — corroborated by the stuck game clearing at the exact same frame as prod.
- The **honesty correction**: the original verdict's claims that the control clears ~100% and that maxFrames=30000 is high-enough / the stuck game is "one real failure prod doesn't share" are **false**. The truth is more favorable to the NN, not less — the single non-clear is a shared cap artifact, conservative, and non-NN-specific — but the verdict must be restated accurately and its internal contradictions (analytic 90/90 vs 17/18) fixed.

So: this is the honest "pure NN that genuinely decides AND succeeds." It meets ≥95% on the pooled metric and ties prod on the hardest cell's lone artifact. It does NOT meet ≥95% as a strict per-cell gate at n=18 on 2560×1440 — but neither does the oracle/prod control, which proves that residual is a harness cap limit, not a deficiency of the NN. **PASS-with-caveats**: accept the model; raise the maxFrames cap (~32000+) and re-run 2560×1440 to convert the per-cell gate to a clean pass, and correct the verdict document's false "~100% control / one real failure" language and the 90/90-vs-17/18 contradiction.
